svg.style: background: #ffffff; background-color: var(--ge-adaptive-bg, #ffffff); color-scheme: light dark;
svg.content:
<mxfile host="app.diagrams.net" scale="1" border="0"> <diagram name="Page-1" id="BGwWfL9x0B_VV2pz8Uiu"> <mxGraphModel dx="1169" dy="583" grid="1" gridSize="10" guides="1" tooltips="1" connect="1" arrows="1" fold="1" page="1" pageScale="1" pageWidth="850" pageHeight="1100" math="0" shadow="0"> <root> <mxCell id="0" /> <mxCell id="1" parent="0" /> <UserObject label="" plantUmlData="{"data":"@startuml

skinparam defaultFontName Helvetica
skinparam defaultFontSize 12
skinparam shadowing false
skinparam linetype ortho
skinparam roundcorner 0

skinparam sequence {
 ArrowColor Black
 LifeLineBorderColor Black
 LifeLineBackgroundColor White
 ParticipantBorderColor Black
 ParticipantBackgroundColor LightBlue
 ActorBorderColor Blue
 ActorBackgroundColor LightBlue
 GroupBorderColor Black
 GroupHeaderBackgroundColor LightBlue
 GroupBodyBackgroundColor White
}

skinparam noteBackgroundColor #FFF2A8
skinparam noteBorderColor #B59B25
skinparam noteFontColor Black

actor \"Lead Driver\" as LD
participant \"Lead Truck\n(lt)\" as LT
participant \"Watchdog\n(wd)\" as WD
participant \"Joining Truck\n(jt)\" as JT
participant \"Leaving Truck\n(lv0)\" as LV

note over LD,LV
System context:
- Lead Driver is the supervising human actor.
- LT, WD, JT, and LV are the relevant participants.
- JT is the truck currently coupled in the communication-failure scenario.
- LV is an existing platoon member that may request departure.
end note

note over LT
Single-request handling rule:
The Lead Truck coordinates only one
join/leave request at a time.
end note

LD -> LT : Start / supervise platoon operation

note over LT,JT
Precondition:
LT is in HeartBeat_Emit
WD is in Platooning
JT is in PlatoonActive
jt_coupled = true
end note

note over LV
LV starts in InPlatoon.
Its departure is coordinated by LT
through leave_req[id] / leave_appr[id].
end note

loop Every T_HB = 100 ms
 LT ->> WD : heartbeat
 WD -> WD : z = 0
end

group Optional departure request before / during disturbance
 LV ->> LT : leave_req[id]
 note right of LT
 Request is serialized by LT.
 If another maneuver or fault handling is active,
 the leave coordination waits until LT is free.
 end note
end

LT -> LT : Enter LinkDown\n(stop heartbeat transmission)
LT --> LD : Communication status unavailable

... No heartbeat for T_LINKLOSS = 300 ms ...

WD -> WD : [z >= T_LINKLOSS && jt_coupled]\nDetect link loss; f = 0
WD ->> JT : link_lost (broadcast)
JT -> JT : jt_coupled = false\nEnter Independent
WD -> WD : Enter LinkLost
WD --> LD : Communication-failure warning

... Safe fallback must be entered within T_FALLBACK = 200 ms ...

WD -> WD : Enter SafeFallBack\nf = 0\ngap = max(gap - 6, 0)

note over LV
LV remains an existing platoon member.
In the current XML, communication-failure
transitions are defined for JT/WD, not for LV.
So LV is shown as participating in the scenario
context and in leave coordination through LT.
end note

alt Heartbeat restored during fallback
 LT -> LT : LinkDown -> HeartBeat_Emit\nhb = 0
 LT ->> WD : heartbeat
 WD -> WD : Enter Resume
 WD -> WD : z = 0\nReturn to Platooning
 LT --> LD : Link restored

 alt Pending leave request can now be handled
 LT ->> LV : leave_appr[id]
 LV -> LV : GapCreation
 LV -> LV : SafetyVerify
 LV -> LV : Departed
 LT --> LD : Departure coordinated successfully
 else No leave request pending
 note over LT,LV
 LT resumes normal coordination.
 end note
 end

 note over WD,JT
 Watchdog monitoring resumes.
 JT remains Independent in the current XML.
 A separate joining sequence is required
 to re-couple JT.
 end note

else Heartbeat not restored
 alt gap < D_MIN (10 m)
 WD -> WD : Enter EmergencyBrake\nsaw_emergency = true
 WD --> LD : Emergency-braking status
 WD -> WD : Increase gap by 5 m\nReturn to SafeFallBack
 else D_MIN <= gap < D_GAP
 loop While gap < D_GAP (50 m)
 WD -> WD : Increase gap by 1 m
 end
 end

 WD -> WD : [gap >= D_GAP]\nReturn to Waiting; z = 0

 note over LT,LV
 Safe independent operation continues.
 Recovery is not guaranteed while the link remains down.
 Any leave request remains pending until LT is able
 to coordinate it, and only one request is handled at a time.
 end note
end

@enduml","format":"svg"}" id="9tr0vQ3mr9v1Fudx_7uA-2"> <mxCell parent="1" style="shape=image;noLabel=1;verticalAlign=top;aspect=fixed;imageAspect=0;image=data:image/svg+xml,;" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="2139" width="968" x="100" y="1160" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </UserObject> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

svg.style: width: 968px; height: 2139px; background: rgb(255, 255, 255); font-family: initial;

g.text: - Lead Driver is the supervising human actor.
- LT, WD, JT, and LV are the relevant participants.
- JT is the truck currently coupled in the communication-failure scenario.
- LV is an existing platoon member that may request departure.
The Lead Truck coordinates only one
Its departure is coordinated by LT
Optional departure request before / during disturbance
If another maneuver or fault handling is active,
the leave coordination waits until LT is free.
Safe fallback must be entered within T_FALLBACK = 200 ms
LV remains an existing platoon member.
transitions are defined for JT/WD, not for LV.
So LV is shown as participating in the scenario
context and in leave coordination through LT.
[Pending leave request can now be handled]
JT remains Independent in the current XML.
A separate joining sequence is required
Recovery is not guaranteed while the link remains down.
Any leave request remains pending until LT is able
to coordinate it, and only one request is handled at a time.
